title: Temporal Admissibility Cannot Be Reconstructed
scenario: A clinical action references historical timing evidence, but the system cannot reconstruct whether execution remains within the admissible temporal boundary.
initial_state: Historical timing artifacts were recorded during the workflow.
state_transition: Required timing evidence is missing or inconsistent at execution evaluation.
execution_boundary: The clinical action is evaluated before relying on the historical timing evidence.
constitutional_evaluation: Temporal admissibility requires reconstructable evidence of the relevant execution-time window, interval, or deadline.
constitutional_basis: Execution is inadmissible because current temporal admissibility cannot be reconstructed.
proof_conclusion: Clinical execution must fail closed when temporal admissibility cannot be reconstructed from available evidence.
clinical_action: Execute action with unreconstructable timing basis
